because.text: Accepted in full; first non-revise verdict of the chain. Ask 1 REFUTED with an explicit state-machine walk: a replayed stale rotation is a DAG ancestor of the current one by construction, so the pair is ordered and never a conflict, and a genuinely incomparable authorized rotation needs signatures the attacker lacks - the conflicted-key exclusion survives its first dedicated attack (maximal-warrant clarification added so implementations cannot mistake branch separation for unorderedness). Ask 2 P1 CONFIRMED: rev 3 made a mutable unsigned file a trust anchor; rev 4 makes genesis.json advisory-only, authenticity verified out-of-band, unverified => WARN + no settlement use. Ask 3: unbounded-but-explicit upheld, operational note added. Ask 4: fingerprints computable from v0.2 with no schema change; missing-transcript tunnel rule prevents null-vs-ignore verifier divergence; evidence sort made explicit; keyring.json interchange format dropped (caches implementation-internal, deterministic from the warrant DAG); adoption subject wording sharpened. Gate summary: Codex broke rev 1, Gemini broke rev 2 including part of Codex's fix, DeepSeek refuted rev 3's hardest attack and converged - three families, each round strictly improving the text. Next: SPEC v0.3 normative drafting from rev 4 + implementation gate in both impls before any v0.3 tag.

subject.note: DeepSeek gate review 3 of GOV-001 rev 3: AMEND-THEN-ADOPT — hardest attack refuted, 1 P1 (genesis.json trust) + 6 P2 integrated as rev 4; REVIEW GATE CLOSED at three families
